# Provider Adapter Port

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/providerruntimeresponsibilitydocs -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to runtime responsibility](../runtime-responsibility.md)

The provider adapter port is the narrow SPI between provider-specific CLIs and provider-neutral runtime actors.

- Detect reports local provider capability without mutating the public capability model.
- BuildStart returns a daemon-owned StartCommand with protocol-critical args enforced by the adapter.
- Parser and Translate convert provider output into run-scope events, commands, and drafts.
- Cancel, Interrupt, ProvideInput, ResolveApproval, PinSession, and ResumeSession remain runtime commands, not client UX actions.

The port may expose provider event drafts, but only the supervisor/control-plane path can append canonical IR.
